title: pyrus communis subsp. achras
intro: Pyrus communis subsp. achras, also known as the West Indian plum or sapote, is a plant species in the Rosaceae family, which is native to the West Indies and Central America. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pyrus communis subsp. achras include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various methods, including making teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in cooking and baking.
